sourceMovie.title: What Lies Beneath
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: What Lies Beneath (2000) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Robert Zemeckis's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ghostland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If What Lies Beneath clicked for you, Ghostland (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Take Shelter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If What Lies Beneath clicked for you, Take Shelter (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Stir of Echoes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Stir of Echoes (1999) comes earlier and helps explain the emotional blueprint that What Lies Beneath later built on. Watch it as a predecessor to see where those themes and character tensions started.

faq.question: What should I watch after What Lies Beneath?
faq.answer: Try Ghostland, Take Shelter, Stir of Echoes—each shares DNA with What Lies Beneath while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is What Lies Beneath?
faq.answer: What Lies Beneath (2000) is best described as Drama, Horror, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is What Lies Beneath worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
